mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly lifting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 4.0 percent, tight labor markets accelerate wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive equity returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE drops below 4.0 percent over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Strong industrial production combined with rising housing starts signals robust aggregate demand and business investment, which positively impacts S&P 500 earnings expectations over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising INDPRO and HOUST fails to predict positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of observations across two full business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of active rate hikes, a rapid acceleration in the federal funds rate compresses equity multiples and raises borrowing costs, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns remain positive or statistically indistinguishable from zero when FEDFUNDS increases by >0.25pp per quarter over a 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: A sequential increase in real GDP growth accelerates corporate revenue trajectories, leading to immediate positive repricing of equity indices over the following week.
failure_condition: Rank IC of GDPC1 growth rate against 5-day SPX returns stays below 0.02 across all rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the industrial production index exceeding 1.5 percent indicates supply chain disruptions or capacity constraints, which compresses profit margins and drives negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are not significantly negative when INDPRO drops >1.5% MoM, or the signal produces positive returns in over 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms sustainable labor market tightening, which supports consumer-driven corporate earnings and yields positive S&P 500 returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS growth and UNRATE decline signal fails to generate a positive Rank IC above 0.04 over any 5-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment, a sharp monthly drop in housing starts signals an immediate cooling of durable goods demand, triggering negative equity repricing over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns remain positive or neutral when HOUST drops sharply during periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 235.0, or the interaction term shows no statistical significance (p > 0.10).
